title: Chapter 26 – Zhongnan Sect
content:
Cheong Myeong turned aside and rummaged through his belongings. Ma Jin and Baek Mi-ryeo exchanged knowing looks.

Before long, Cheong Myeong produced his own blue long robe and approached Jeong Yeon-shin.

The garment unfurled wide as it draped over him.

Jeong Yeon-shin glanced at the shoulder width of the blue robe now covering his frame and looked up, clearly puzzled.
It was Baek Mi-ryeo who answered.

Rising Star. In the martial world, this title was not given lightly. It carried real weight.

It designated the finest among the younger generation walking the martial path.

That weight doubled given it had been bestowed less than half a year after entering Desolate Sect.

She smiled — a captivating expression without a trace of self-interest.
Cheong Myeong grinned and turned to Jeong Yeon-shin.

Jeong Yeon-shin bowed slightly. The unfamiliar warmth stirring in his chest felt strange.

Ma Jin, who had been watching from the side with a somewhat melancholic expression, gave a nod.
At that moment, Hyeon Won-chang raised his hand with care.
Ma Jin considered it briefly, then agreed — it was a reasonable point.

Jeong Yeon-shin and Hyeon Won-chang clasped their fists in salute.

Jeong Yeon-shin's gaze, now sharper than before, turned toward the southern window.

Zhongnan Mountain — where one of the Central Plains' celebrated sword sects had built its stronghold.

The place where his second brother and young niece were studying martial arts. He wondered if Hye-ah was doing well.

'The annihilation of the Jeong family. Perhaps Elder Yeo Il-sin can tell me the truth, if I find her.'

The war between the Thirteen Heavens and the Nine Sects. It was also a chance to see how his martial power measured against the wider world.

* * *

Zhongnan Mountain was two days' journey away. The pair changed into Desolate Sect martial uniforms after sleeping two nights on the road.

It was a gesture of respect toward the Mount Hua Sect's reputation.

Desolate Junior Hyeon Won-chang broke into a refreshing laugh.

He glanced between the '荒' characters on both his shoulders and let the corners of his mouth rise.

Jeong Yeon-shin wore the blue long robe Cheong Myeong had given him over his white Desolate Sect uniform.

Hyeon Won-chang looked at him with open envy.

Jeong Yeon-shin allowed himself a slight smile.

He was a man worth being grateful for. It wouldn't be easy for someone without genuine affection to volunteer for a mission unrelated to their own duties.

Before they knew it, the two had arrived at the entrance of Zhongnan Mountain.

From a distance, the steep and solemn mountain terrain had filled them with awe, but standing at its threshold, they felt something else entirely — the unmistakable severity of a martial arts sect.

Martial artists stood guard at the entrance.

Hyeon Won-chang whispered.

As the two drew closer, two of the four guards stepped forward.

Hyeon Won-chang replied at ease, subtly pushing out his shoulder to show the emblem, and sent a glance toward Jeong Yeon-shin.

The message was clear: take the lead as a Desolate Sect rising star.

At the mention of Desolate Sect, even the guards standing in the rear moved closer. Jeong Yeon-shin spoke.

The guards examined their clothing down to the last detail and let out a low murmur.

Their reaction told the whole story.

Given that Desolate Sect existed to police the martial world, it was not a welcome presence among the great orthodox sects.

One of the Zhongnan disciples spoke. His tone carried a sharp edge.

The two from Radiant Demon Wing produced golden identity tokens from inside their robes.

The Desolate tokens appeared in their hands — gold-plated, intricately engraved wooden pieces.

They were said to be forged by dwarf clan blacksmiths rarer than any famous artisan.

The delicately and elegantly carved patterns left no room for doubt.
The disciple with the sharp tone was pulled back by someone older. A senior martial artist stepped forward.

Hyeon Won-chang bristled and released his energy. The wave of force that rippled faintly through the air spread with unmistakable clarity.

He had passed the Desolate Exam cleanly and had since proven himself as a Radiant Demon Wing white-level warrior.

Beneath his usually lighthearted manner, the man had genuine substance.
While Hyeon Won-chang savored their startled reaction, the Zhongnan disciple who had been pushed back shouted.

Hyeon Won-chang's expression twisted with exasperation.

Jeong Yeon-shin, watching them steadily, felt a pang of recognition.

He thought of the Jeong family.

When they had declared they would create and study martial arts, the first thing that appeared on his brothers' faces was not bewilderment but something else entirely.

Surprise and jealousy. These disciples were no different.

'The war with the Tyrant Sword Sect must not be going well. Their pride as one of the Nine Sects is fracturing, and now two young strangers arrive claiming to be confident Desolate Sect martial artists.'

Hyeon Won-chang's display of force had actually been counterproductive.

The martial artists of the Nine Sects were famed for their deep cultivation and composure.

But the circumstances and the heat of youth had stripped these Zhongnan disciples of that composure, leaving them no different from ordinary hotheaded fighters.

His tone grew more resolute. Jeong Yeon-shin felt a tightness in his chest.

Guarding the gate was an important duty, but it was not one entrusted to the sect's senior members.

All those serving as mountain gate and entrance guards were young.

At their age, they brimmed with vigor, and the pride of belonging to a prestigious sect like the Zhongnan only amplified it.

Had thoughts of his own kin crossed his mind?

In that instant, he was glad to see Hyeon Won-chang swallowing his anger with a sideways glance in his direction. Jeong Yeon-shin was about to step forward when—

The one who had remained silent until now spoke with undisguised contempt.

The force behind his slanted eyes was no ordinary thing.

He had clearly crossed a line. Jeong Yeon-shin's hand moved to the sword at his waist.

He spoke calmly.

* * *

Jeong Jung-san was a young man with a rough-hewn face, dressed in a cotton robe.

He was the second son of the Jeong family and a disciple of the Zhongnan Sect.

Lately, he had been turning over in his mind how things had come to this.

Even if the annihilation of the Jeong family had been like a natural disaster, the hearts of people were another matter entirely.

Though he was no Buddhist disciple, he found himself thinking about karma.

He had been talking to himself more often.

He was leaning against the roof ridge of the Zhongnan Sect's Seeking Pavilion.

The view before him was utterly peaceful.

An azure sky where light feathery clouds drifted in gentle waves, the mountain ranges of Qinling stretching endlessly below, and a handful of bamboo stalks rising above the fence.

He tapped his foot idly against the pavilion's roof.

The clear sound of stone striking stone gave him a small measure of satisfaction.

At the same moment, an aged voice erupted from below.

He leapt down at once and bowed his head toward the source of the scolding.

An old swordsman with a stern expression sat in the main hall, regarding him with displeasure.

It was the kind of statement only someone of his stature could make. Jeong Jung-san bowed even lower.

Although the Zhongnan Sect was locked in battle with the Tyrant Sword Sect, he was not a martial artist who ranked among the Nine Sects' fighters.

He had naturally ended up attending to the elder instead.

The old man clicked his tongue.
At the woman's voice, the old man's expression softened perceptibly.

Jeong Jung-san's eyes widened as she approached. She was around twenty — her appearance poised at the threshold between girlhood and womanhood.

Her striking features and clear eyes were arresting.

What caught his attention first was the unusual feather ornament binding her jet-black hair.

Her wide blue martial arts uniform rippled in the wind as she walked.

A white sword hung from her jade-adorned belt.

The Zhongnan Sect's Wei Ji Myo-hwa.

Also known as the Sword Dragon in the Clouds.

In an era where the title "dragon" was reserved for men.

This was precisely why Jeong Jung-san, who had never laid eyes on the Sword Dragon before, used to assume the title belonged to a great senior brother.

Wei Ji Myo-hwa was something else entirely. The Zhongnan Sect's foremost rising star — a woman who had earned a dragon's name.

For Jeong Jung-san, she was a figure so far above him he could scarcely meet her gaze.

She half-mumbled his name as though it barely registered, then turned on her heel.

When the old man gestured impatiently for him to follow, Jeong Jung-san scrambled after Wei Ji Myo-hwa.

They exchanged not a single word on the way down to the entrance of Zhongnan Mountain.

Jeong Jung-san found her intimidating — her reputation towered despite her youth — and Wei Ji Myo-hwa seemed entirely indifferent to his existence.

Jeong Jung-san suddenly made an involuntary sound. He nearly rubbed his eyes, as though confronted by the impossible.

He saw his senior brothers receiving guests alongside Wei Ji Myo-hwa, who had stopped abruptly.

The atmosphere was growing visibly tense, but that was not what seized Jeong Jung-san's attention.
It was the first time Wei Ji Myo-hwa had shown any interest.

The scene before his eyes was nothing short of shocking.

Without even seeing the sword drawn, his own brother was already sheathing his blade — and the senior brother's sword was spinning through the air.

A savage gust erupted from the side. Wei Ji Myo-hwa had stomped the ground and launched herself forward.
